title: DSR effective-N: deflate by independent-trial count (clustering / rho-bar), not raw product
source: LdP newer-corpus verification (4 parallel read-only agents over the added 2012-2026 papers + LdP REFERENCE CODE in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/code, 2026-06-03). Conversation: 'verifica la nueva informacion antes de implementar'.
why: B0132 deflates DSR by raw N = models x folds x thresholds (a per-primary lower bound). Verification vs the 2014 DSR paper + 2026 'new standard' found LdP prescribes the EFFECTIVE NUMBER OF INDEPENDENT trials: N = rho_bar + (1-rho_bar)*M (collapses to 1 as trials -> perfectly correlated), or derived via CLUSTERING of the trial-correlation matrix (2026: 'K = effective number of independent trials, via clustering'). Folds and threshold-grid points are highly correlated, so our raw product OVERSTATES independence even as it under-counts cross-program trials.
value_rationale: Makes the DSR trial count the canonical effective-independent count, not a correlated-inflated raw product. Reuses the ONC machinery from B0139.
scope: Compute effective N from the trial-Sharpe correlation matrix (rho-bar interpolation N=rho_bar+(1-rho_bar)*M, or ONC cluster count)
Feed effective N to deflated_sharpe_ratio's n_trials; keep the lower-bound label until done
Reuse ONC (B0139) clustering on the trial cross-correlation
recommendation: Refine B0132 once ONC (B0139) lands (shared clustering machinery).
out_of_scope: The PSR/DSR formula itself (verified byte-exact, no change).

external_refs.label: effective independent trials via clustering; 2014 N=rho_bar+(1-rho_bar)M

history.reason: verification: DSR N should be effective-independent (clustering/rho-bar), not raw product
